questions.q: What does the abbreviation PSLV stand for?
questions.explanation: PSLV stands for Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle, ISRO's expendable medium-lift rocket.

questions.q: Which space agency developed and operates the PSLV?
questions.explanation: The PSLV was designed, built and is operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.q: The PSLV is famously nicknamed which of the following?
questions.explanation: Because of its reliability and many missions, the PSLV is widely called 'the Workhorse of ISRO'.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the PSLV?
questions.explanation: The PSLV is a launch vehicle whose job is to place satellites into orbit around Earth.

questions.q: The PSLV was especially designed to deliver satellites into what kind of orbit?
questions.explanation: As its name suggests, the PSLV was built primarily to place remote-sensing satellites into Sun-synchronous polar orbits.

questions.q: Which country built the PSLV?
questions.explanation: The PSLV is an indigenous Indian rocket developed by ISRO.

questions.q: The PSLV is best described as which type of rocket?
questions.explanation: The PSLV is an expendable launch vehicle, meaning its stages are not recovered and reused.

questions.q: From which Indian spaceport are PSLV rockets launched?
questions.explanation: PSLV missions lift off from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre at Sriharikota in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: The PSLV has launched satellites for India and for which other group?
questions.explanation: The PSLV has launched hundreds of satellites including many foreign and commercial payloads from numerous countries.

questions.q: Roughly how many stages does the standard PSLV have?
questions.explanation: The PSLV is a four-stage rocket that alternates solid and liquid propulsion stages.

questions.q: In which year did the PSLV achieve its first fully successful launch?
questions.explanation: After a failed maiden flight in 1993, the PSLV's first fully successful launch came in October 1994 with PSLV-D2.

questions.q: The PSLV is distinctive for alternating which two types of propulsion in its stages?
questions.explanation: The PSLV uses an alternating arrangement of solid-propellant and liquid-propellant stages across its four stages.

questions.q: Which famous lunar mission was launched by a PSLV?
questions.explanation: Chandrayaan-1, India's first lunar mission, was launched in 2008 by a PSLV (the PSLV-XL variant).

questions.q: Which interplanetary mission was launched by a PSLV in 2013?
questions.explanation: India's Mars Orbiter Mission, Mangalyaan, was launched by a PSLV-XL in November 2013.

questions.q: In February 2017, a single PSLV set a world record by launching how many satellites in one flight?
questions.explanation: On 15 February 2017 PSLV-C37 launched a then-record 104 satellites in a single mission.

questions.q: What is the name of the heavier, strap-on enhanced version of the PSLV?
questions.explanation: The PSLV-XL uses larger, more powerful strap-on boosters to carry heavier payloads, as used for Chandrayaan-1 and Mangalyaan.

questions.q: Solid-propellant strap-on boosters are clustered around the first stage of which PSLV variant?
questions.explanation: The standard PSLV and PSLV-XL use six strap-on solid boosters, while the PSLV-CA ('Core Alone') variant flies without strap-ons.

questions.q: The PSLV's first stage uses what kind of motor that is among the largest of its type?
questions.explanation: The PSLV first stage (PS1) is powered by one of the world's largest solid-propellant rocket motors.

questions.q: Which navigation satellites for India's regional system (NavIC/IRNSS) were largely launched by the PSLV?
questions.explanation: Most satellites of India's IRNSS/NavIC regional navigation constellation were placed in orbit by the PSLV.

questions.q: Which Indian space telescope/astronomy satellite was launched by a PSLV in 2015?
questions.explanation: AstroSat, India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory, was launched by a PSLV in September 2015.

questions.q: The PSLV's third stage is powered by what type of propellant?
questions.explanation: The PSLV's third stage (PS3) is a solid-propellant motor, part of its alternating solid-liquid stage design.

questions.q: Approximately what mass can the standard PSLV deliver to a Sun-synchronous polar orbit?
questions.explanation: The PSLV can typically deliver roughly 1,000-1,750 kg to a Sun-synchronous polar orbit, depending on the variant.

questions.q: What designation was given to the first, partially failed developmental flight of the PSLV in 1993?
questions.explanation: The maiden developmental flight in 1993, designated PSLV-D1, failed to reach orbit due to a control/software issue.

questions.q: The fourth stage (PS4) of the PSLV is powered by what kind of engines?
questions.explanation: The PSLV fourth stage (PS4) uses twin Earth-storable liquid-propellant engines for precise orbital insertion.

questions.q: What experimental capability did ISRO add to the PS4 stage, demonstrated as the 'PS4 Orbital Platform' (POEM)?
options: Using the spent fourth stage as an in-orbit experiment platform
questions.explanation: ISRO turned the spent PS4 stage into an orbital platform (POEM) to host experiments after its primary payloads are deployed.

questions.q: Which PSLV variant launched the record 104-satellite mission and Mangalyaan, distinguished by its larger strap-ons?
questions.explanation: The PSLV-XL, with its extended (larger) strap-on boosters, launched both Mangalyaan and the 104-satellite PSLV-C37 mission.

questions.q: Newer PSLV variants such as PSLV-DL and PSLV-QL differ mainly in what respect?
options: The number of strap-on boosters used
questions.explanation: PSLV-DL flies with two strap-on boosters and PSLV-QL with four, configurations tailored to different payload needs.

questions.q: ISRO's first satellite to study the Sun, launched by a PSLV in September 2023, was named what?
questions.explanation: Aditya-L1, India's first solar observation mission, was launched aboard a PSLV-XL in September 2023.

questions.q: Apart from a 1993 failure, the PSLV's reliability was briefly interrupted by which later mission's heat-shield problem?
questions.explanation: The PSLV-C39 mission in August 2017 failed when the payload fairing (heat shield) did not separate, trapping the satellite.

questions.q: The PSLV's success was crucial in advancing which broader Indian space capability?
options: Building a self-reliant satellite launch and remote-sensing program
questions.explanation: By reliably orbiting remote-sensing, navigation and scientific satellites, the PSLV underpinned India's self-reliant space program.
